course_outcomes.LO1: Analyze the efficiency of algorithms using asymptotic notation.
course_outcomes.LO2: Design algorithms using major paradigms such as divide-and-conquer, greedy algorithms, and dynamic programming.
course_outcomes.LO3: Compare algorithms and evaluate trade-offs in performance, complexity, and implementation.
course_outcomes.LO4: Prove algorithm correctness using formal proof techniques including induction, exchange arguments, and loop invariants.
course_outcomes.LO5: Identify NP-complete problems and reason about reductions and intractability.
course_outcomes.LO6: Implement efficient algorithms in a high-level programming language.
course_outcomes.LO7: Communicate technical ideas clearly in written explanations, proofs, and collaborative work.

program_outcomes.SO1: Analyze a complex computing problem and apply principles of computing and other relevant disciplines to identify solutions.
program_outcomes.SO2: Design, implement, and evaluate a computing-based solution to meet a given set of computing requirements in the context of the program's discipline.
program_outcomes.SO3: Communicate effectively in a variety of professional contexts.
program_outcomes.SO6: Apply computer science theory and software development fundamentals to produce computing-based solutions.
